pet.name: Biscuit
pet.special_needs:
Large senior dog with weight management needs, slower walks, joint
  support, and consistent hydration monitoring.
pet.characteristics: Dogs require daily exercise scaled to age and size, consistent training and socialization, regular veterinary checkups, dental care, and mental enrichment through play and interaction. Diet should be portion-controlled and age-appropriate, with fresh water available continuously. Senior dogs benefit from orthopedic bedding, gentle movement, and monitoring for cognitive and mobility changes. Large-breed dogs typically have shorter lifespans than small breeds and are prone to joint and heart issues; weight management and joint support become increasingly important with age. Environmental comfort, routine, and preventive healthcare support quality of life.
pet.species_profile_summary: Typical dog lifespan ranges 10-13 years; large breeds trend toward the lower end, and senior dogs require attentive monitoring of mobility, hydration, weight, and joint health.

age_context.summary: Typical dog lifespan ranges 10-13 years; large breeds trend toward the lower end, and senior dogs require attentive monitoring of mobility, hydration, weight, and joint health.

retrieved_passages.title: Senior Pet Support
retrieved_passages.content: Senior pets often need lower-impact exercise, more frequent comfort checks, and closer observation of appetite, hydration, and mobility. Shorter walks, joint-friendly movement, and calm enrichment are often easier to tolerate than intense activity.

retrieved_passages.title: Enrichment and Daily Monitoring
retrieved_passages.content: Daily care plans are stronger when they combine essential care with realistic enrichment and observation. Short play, sniff walks, brushing, or calm interaction can improve quality of life without requiring large blocks of time.

retrieved_passages.title: Enrichment and Daily Monitoring
retrieved_passages.content: Observation tasks are useful for special-needs pets because they create a record of appetite, water intake, energy, and bathroom habits. These tasks should be short, specific, and easy to complete consistently.

retrieved_passages.title: Puppy and Kitten Routine Basics
retrieved_passages.content: Exercise and enrichment for very young pets should stay short and positive. Puppies often do better with several brief walks or training moments instead of one long outing, and kittens benefit from short interactive play sessions that stop before exhaustion.

retrieved_passages.title: Senior Pet Support
retrieved_passages.content: A senior pet plan should prioritize consistency over intensity. When time is limited, essential care such as feeding, hydration, medication reminders, and mobility support should be scheduled before optional enrichment.

raw_recommendations.name: Morning hydration check and fresh water refill
raw_recommendations.notes: Ensure fresh water is available and observe water intake. Senior dogs with weight management needs benefit from consistent hydration monitoring.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Hydration is critical for senior pets and supports joint health and overall function. Daily observation creates a baseline for detecting changes.

raw_recommendations.name: Gentle low-impact walk or sniff walk
raw_recommendations.notes: Keep pace slow and distance manageable. Allow frequent stops for sniffing and rest. Avoid stairs or uneven terrain when possible.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Senior large dogs need scaled, joint-friendly movement. Shorter walks are easier to tolerate and reduce strain on aging joints.

raw_recommendations.name: Feeding with portion control
raw_recommendations.notes: Measure portions carefully for weight management. Monitor appetite and eating behavior. Use age-appropriate, portion-controlled diet.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Weight management is essential for senior large dogs to reduce joint stress. Consistent feeding times and portion control support this goal.

raw_recommendations.name: Afternoon calm enrichment or gentle interaction
raw_recommendations.notes: Offer quiet play, brushing, or sniff-based activities indoors. Avoid high-impact games. Suitable for rainy days.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Calm enrichment improves quality of life without exhausting a senior dog. Short, realistic activities are easier to maintain consistently.

raw_recommendations.name: Evening mobility and comfort check
raw_recommendations.notes: Observe gait, stiffness, and comfort level. Note any changes in mobility or signs of joint discomfort. Ensure orthopedic bedding is accessible.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Close observation of mobility and comfort is essential for senior dogs. Creates a record of changes that may indicate joint or health issues.

raw_recommendations.name: Weekly appetite and bathroom habit review
raw_recommendations.notes: Review notes from the week on appetite, water intake, energy level, and bathroom habits. Look for patterns or changes.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Observation tasks create a health record for senior pets. Consistent monitoring helps detect early signs of health changes.

raw_recommendations.name: Weekly gentle brushing and skin check
raw_recommendations.notes: Brush gently to avoid discomfort. Check skin, coat, and paws for any abnormalities. Provides calm interaction and comfort.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Grooming supports circulation and allows observation of skin and coat health. Calm brushing is enriching for senior dogs.

raw_recommendations.name: Monthly weight and body condition assessment
raw_recommendations.notes: Track weight trends and body condition. Note any significant changes. Share observations with veterinarian at checkups.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Weight management is critical for senior large dogs to reduce joint stress and support mobility. Monthly tracking helps ensure diet adjustments are working.
